company_core.products: Noma Security offers an AI security and governance platform designed to provide visibility and control throughout the AI lifecycle, from model development to application runtime. The platform utilizes API-based integrations and real-time threat detection to manage security and compliance risks in AI applications across various environments.

founders.name: Niv Braun
founders.background:
Niv Braun served as a Group Manager at Verint from 2020 to 2022, where he led a 50-person team in developing a new cybersecurity product line. Prior to that, he held various roles at Verint, including Product Team Lead and Outbound Product Manager. His experience also includes serving as a Cyber Security Group Manager in the Israeli Military Intelligence - Unit 8200 from 2015 to 2016.

---
Sources: linkedin.com/in/niv-braun

founders.name: Alon Tron
founders.background:
Alon Tron served as the Data Science Lead at Interai from 2020 to 2022, where he utilized machine learning models and computer vision algorithms to enhance enterprise applications. Prior to that, he held multiple roles at Israeli Military Intelligence, including Development Consultant and Tech Lead in Unit 8200, where he guided development teams in technology upgrades and requirement design. He holds dual Bachelor of Science degrees in Computer Science and Computational Biology from UC San Diego and The Hebrew University of Jerusalem.

---
Sources: linkedin.com/in/alon-tron

Additionally, they are collaborating with AWS to establish a secure foundation for enterprise AI, indicating growth potential in the AI sector.

agent_output.subject: Noma's AI security architecture
agent_output.message:
Hi Niv,

By way of introduction, my name is Danielle and I'm a partner at NEA. We've been grateful to be Series A / B lead investors in companies like Plaid, Databricks, Robinhood, Cloudflare, and many others.

I've been spending time understanding the AI security landscape, and Noma's approach to lifecycle visibility caught my attention. The API-based integration model you've built is particularly interesting — most solutions we've seen focus narrowly on either model development or runtime, but rarely provide continuous coverage across both. I'm curious how you're handling the handoff between development-time governance and production runtime monitoring, especially as models move between environments.

The AWS collaboration and Pisani joining to lead the agentic AI push suggest you're thinking several steps ahead on where enterprise AI security needs to go. I'd love to understand more about your roadmap here and how you're thinking about the governance challenges that emerge as companies move from isolated AI experiments to production agentic workflows.

Would you be open to a conversation? Happy to work around your schedule.

NEA is a venture capital firm. We lead early rounds in big ideas and work with generational founders. We're partners to companies such as Perplexity (Series A lead), Databricks (Series B lead), Plaid (Series A lead), Robinhood (Series B lead), Cloudflare (Series B lead), and many others. Our team is based in Menlo Park, CA and NYC.

Danielle
